commands.repair_reason: structured ClawSweeper needs-human verdict with repairable P-severity findings (sha=6aded510eec09d6056cdd45b03282603e5ea6e4d)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds a bundled `xiaomi-token-plan` provider with regional auth/onboarding choices, static catalog rows, config and usage wiring, Xiaomi docs, and focused tests.

PR surface: Source +545, Tests +285, Docs +76. Total +906 across 22 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the missing capability: current main and v2026.5.22 only expose the pay-as-you-go `xiaomi` provider and `XIAOMI_API_KEY` auth path. This is a feature gap rather than a broken existing contract.
commands.review_followup: This needs maintainer provider/auth contract approval and dirty-branch resolution, not an autonomous ClawSweeper repair lane.

target.title: [codex] Add Xiaomi Token Plan provider support
target.body:
## Summary

- add first-class `xiaomi-token-plan` support to the bundled Xiaomi provider plugin
- add regional Token Plan onboarding/auth choices, static v2.5 catalog entries, and `tp-...` / `sk-...` key-shape validation
- wire the new provider through usage/auth/config overlay surfaces and update Xiaomi provider docs

## Why

OpenClaw's bundled Xiaomi plugin only supported pay-as-you-go `xiaomi` setup, while issue #86169 asked for Xiaomi MiMo Token Plan support without requiring manual custom-provider wiring.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/xiaomi/index.test.ts extensions/xiaomi/onboard.test.ts src/commands/auth-choice-options.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/auth-choice.contract.test.ts src/config/config-misc.test.ts src/infra/provider-usage.shared.test.ts src/infra/provider-usage.load.test.ts src/infra/provider-usage.auth.normalizes-keys.test.ts`
- `node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check src/infra/provider-usage.types.ts src/infra/provider-usage.shared.ts src/config/zod-schema.core.ts src/infra/provider-usage.shared.test.ts src/config/config-misc.test.ts src/infra/provider-usage.load.test.ts src/infra/provider-usage.auth.normalizes-keys.test.ts extensions/xiaomi/index.ts extensions/xiaomi/index.test.ts extensions/xiaomi/onboard.ts extensions/xiaomi/onboard.test.ts extensions/xiaomi/provider-catalog.ts extensions/xiaomi/openclaw.plugin.json extensions/xiaomi/stream.ts extensions/xiaomi/thinking.ts src/commands/auth-choice-options.test.ts`
- `node scripts/format-docs.mjs --check`
- `git diff --check`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: OpenClaw can onboard and resolve Xiaomi MiMo Token Plan as a first-class provider with regional preset endpoints instead of requiring manual custom-provider wiring.

Real environment tested: macOS local source checkout with live Xiaomi Token Plan China (`cn`) region API key. Tested both direct `/v1/models` and `/v1/chat/completions` endpoints, plus full openclaw embedded agent integration through the xiaomi-token-plan provider.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
1. Direct API test: `curl -s "https://token-plan-cn.xiaomimimo.com/v1/models"` — returned 8 models (mimo-v2-omni, mimo-v2-pro, mimo-v2-tts, mimo-v2.5, mimo-v2.5-pro, mimo-v2.5-tts, mimo-v2.5-tts-voiceclone, mimo-v2.5-tts-voicedesign). HTTP 200.
2. Chat completion `mimo-v2.5-pro`: `curl -s "https://token-plan-cn.xiaomimimo.com/v1/chat/completions" -d '{"model":"mimo-v2.5-pro","messages":[{"role":"user","content":"Say hello in one sentence."}],"max_tokens":50}'` — returned valid chat completion with reasoning_content. HTTP 200.
3. Chat completion `mimo-v2.5`: `curl -s "https://token-plan-cn.xiaomimimo.com/v1/chat/completions" -d '{"model":"mimo-v2.5","messages":[{"role":"user","content":"Reply with exactly: hello world"}],"max_tokens":50}'` — returned "hello world". HTTP 200.
4. OpenClaw embedded agent with `mimo-v2.5`: `openclaw agent --local --agent main --model "xiaomi-token-plan/mimo-v2.5" --message "Reply with exactly: hello from token plan" --thinking off --json` — returned `"text": "hello from token plan"`, provider `xiaomi-token-plan`, model `mimo-v2.5`, stop reason `stop`, no fallback used.
5. OpenClaw embedded agent with `mimo-v2.5-pro`: `openclaw agent --local --agent main --model "xiaomi-token-plan/mimo-v2.5-pro" --message "What is 2+2? Answer in one word." --thinking off --json` — returned `"text": "Four"`, provider `xiaomi-token-plan`, model `mimo-v2.5-pro`, stop reason `stop`, no fallback used.

Evidence after fix: `xiaomi-token-plan` provider resolves correctly through the auth profile store, catalogs both Token Plan models (mimo-v2.5-pro, mimo-v2.5), makes successful /v1/chat/completions calls with reasoning and non-reasoning models, and the embedded agent correctly routes `xiaomi-token-plan/model` references without fallback. Unit tests: 8 files passed, 166 tests passed. Format and docs checks all passed.

Observed result after fix: All 5 live API and agent integration tests passed end-to-end. The provider correctly resolves the `cn` region base URL (`token-plan-cn.xiaomimimo.com/v1`) from the provisioned config, authenticates with a `tp-...` key, and returns valid chat completions through the OpenAI-compatible API with working prompt caching (observed cache hits in usage data). Both `mimo-v2.5` and `mimo-v2.5-pro` models respond correctly.

What was not tested: The `ams` (Europe) and `sgp` (Singapore) regional endpoints, real-time streaming behavior, multi-turn conversations with the Token Plan provider, GitHub Actions / Crabbox / Testbox CI runs.

Fixes #86169.
target.author: nianjiuzst
